# serval_approval_delegation (Resource)



## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "serval_approval_delegation" "example_approval_delegation" {
  delegates = [{
    id = "id"
    type = "APPROVAL_DELEGATE_TYPE_UNSPECIFIED"
  }]
  delegator_user_id = "delegatorUserId"
  description = "description"
  priority = 0
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `delegates` (Attributes List) The delegates who can approve on behalf of the delegator. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--delegates))

### Optional

- `delegator_user_id` (String) The ID of the user who is delegating their approvals. When omitted, defaults to the authenticated user.
- `description` (String) A description of the delegation rule.
- `priority` (Number) The priority of the delegation rule (lower values are higher priority).

### Read-Only

- `created_at` (String) The timestamp when the delegation was created.
- `id` (String) The ID of the approval delegation.

<a id="nestedatt--delegates"></a>
### Nested Schema for `delegates`

Optional:

- `id` (String) The ID of the delegate (user ID or group ID, depending on type).
- `type` (String) The type of delegate (user or group).
Available values: "APPROVAL_DELEGATE_TYPE_UNSPECIFIED", "APPROVAL_DELEGATE_TYPE_USER", "APPROVAL_DELEGATE_TYPE_GROUP".

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
$ terraform import serval_approval_delegation.example '<id>'
```
